blurb: Atlanta Streets Alive turns a stretch of the Beltline into a car-free zone for biking, walking, skating, playing, and general wandering. No agenda required. Just show up, pick a direction, and go. It happens twice in this stretch (May 31 and June 14), but May 31 is the one to circle first. It is a great excuse to finally get down to the Beltline if you have been putting it off, or to go back if it has been a while. Free, open to everyone, and genuinely fun for a mix of ages.
scoring_notes: The Beltline is popular with the East Cobb demographic (active, higher income, educated). Streets Alive is a recurring event twice per season, which hurts uniqueness. However, May 31 is within the newsletter window and the event has real appeal. Drive from East Cobb is manageable. Good fit for both families and adult couples.

blurb: The Georgia Renaissance Festival is in its final weekends of the season, which means this is your last chance to catch it before it packs up until next spring. Think jousting, giant turkey legs, costume characters, artisan vendors, and the kind of over-the-top commitment to a bit that you can only appreciate in person. It runs through May 31, so the clock is actually ticking. It is a bit of a drive from East Cobb, but for a Saturday with kids or a group of friends who are up for something different, it delivers. Go before it is gone for another year.
scoring_notes: The end-of-season urgency is a real hook. Families with teens and younger kids will enjoy it. Empty nesters who like quirky experiences can also enjoy it. Fairburn is a longer drive from East Cobb, which is a trade-off. The closing-weekend angle creates genuine scarcity. Annual event but the limited remaining dates give it a can't-miss feel for this newsletter cycle.

date: Saturday (date approximate based on source context)
blurb: This one is genuinely unlike anything else happening in the county this time of year. The Acworth-Cobb Dragon Boat Festival puts 46-foot ornamental boats on Lake Allatoona for races rooted in ancient Chinese tradition, with a drummer on board each boat setting the pace for the paddlers. It is loud, colorful, and completely unlike a typical Saturday afternoon. The event also supports local cancer patients and survivors, so there is real heart behind it. Come watch the races, enjoy the lake, and check off something you have probably never done before.
scoring_notes: High uniqueness score — dragon boat racing is not a common local event and the spectator experience is genuinely distinctive. The charitable component adds emotional appeal. Free to spectate works for any income level. East Cobb audience will appreciate the novelty and the outdoor lakeside setting. Drive to Acworth is manageable. Date is not confirmed precisely from the source — editor should verify.

blurb: A short drive up the road, Wills Park turns into one of the best outdoor art weekends of the spring. The Alpharetta Arts Streetfest brings together artisans, vendors, exhibitors, street performers, and kids activities all in one place across two full days. It is the kind of event where you wander, find something you did not know you needed, and end up staying longer than you planned. Bring the family, bring a friend, or just go solo and enjoy the weather. Free to get in, easy to spend a Saturday doing.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for East Cobb's educated, upper-middle-income audience who appreciates arts and outdoor festivals. Free admission removes any friction. Family-friendly but also fully enjoyable for empty nesters. Not a once-in-a-year event (it recurs annually) but it is a two-day ticketed-window event with real scarcity. Close proximity to East Cobb is a plus.

event_name: Glover Park Concert Series Opening Night
blurb: Forty years of Friday nights at Glover Park, and they are kicking off the 2026 season with Yacht Rock Schooners, the 70s smooth rock group that basically turns every outdoor concert into a summer party. Bring a blanket or a chair, grab something from the Square beforehand, and settle in for a free night out right in your backyard. This is the first concert of a six-month run, which means the energy of opening night plus a crowd that has been waiting all winter for exactly this. Doors at dusk, music at 8. Show up early if you want a good spot.
scoring_notes: Excellent demographic fit. East Cobb's median age of 42 skews squarely toward the Yacht Rock audience (70s smooth rock). Marietta Square is close and familiar to East Cobb readers. Free removes all friction. Opening night of a 40-year-old series has genuine event energy. The series is recurring weekly through summer, but the opening night is a specific one-time moment. Date needs editor confirmation that the 'this Friday' referenced in the source falls on or after May 22.
